Phase-39.NAME: MTProto Telegram Availability Closure
Phase-39.Goal: Close the remaining Telegram Desktop unavailable state by proving the full issued-link path, fixing stale DE runtime fallback config, and improving the user connection flow without rotating existing MTProto secrets or redesigning deployment scripts.

OperatorProblem.item-1: Verified users receive a personal MTProto proxy in the cabinet, but Telegram Desktop still reports the proxy as unavailable.
OperatorProblem.item-2: Phase-38 live evidence proves public HTTPS, admin 8443, RU HAProxy routing, DE listener 443, and private policy health, but not final Telegram client availability.
OperatorProblem.item-3: Live DE logs showed tls_invalid_digest during operator attempts, while policy apply and fake-TLS derivation parity are healthy.

LiveFindings.finding-1: RU database has an active issued assignment and backend repeatedly applies its SNI policy successfully to DE.
LiveFindings.finding-2: RU HAProxy logs show Telegram attempts routed to mtproto_de_runtime, so DNS and SNI routing are not the primary blocker.
LiveFindings.finding-3: DE runtime health reports healthy policy table and policy_count above zero.
LiveFindings.finding-4: Python KrotPN secret derivation and Erlang mtp_fake_tls derivation matched for the issued SNI; a generated fake-TLS ClientHello received a response through public RU 443.
LiveFindings.finding-5: DE runtime env still contained stale DE_MTPROTO_DOMAIN_FRONTING=127.0.0.1:8443 from an earlier deploy, while current Phase-38 contract uses 9443.

NonGoals.item-1: No reimplementation of KPprotoN or mtproto_proxy internals.
NonGoals.item-2: No rotation of MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, SNI names, assignment ids, or tg links.
NonGoals.item-3: No redesign of install.sh or deploy-on-server.sh; only narrow stale-config guards are allowed.
NonGoals.item-4: No public exposure of DE policy API or secret-bearing diagnostics.

DataFlow.flow-1: User opens cabinet and receives owner-only MTProto payload from M-043/M-045 after policy apply through M-044.
DataFlow.flow-2: Primary connection action uses a Telegram web/deep link built from the same server, port, and derived secret.
DataFlow.flow-3: Telegram Desktop connects to issued u-*.krotpn.xyz on public 443, RU HAProxy sends it to DE runtime, and DE mtproto_proxy validates fake-TLS against the per-SNI derived secret.
DataFlow.flow-4: DE runtime connects to Telegram downstream DC using bootstrap config or safe fallback and completes proxy availability checks.
DataFlow.flow-5: Operator diagnostics correlate assignment, policy, route, fake-TLS accept, and downstream status using redacted identifiers only.

Steps.step-1: Create M-051 and V-M-051 as the availability closure contract for Phase-39.
Steps.step-2: Add a secret-free local/static availability smoke for link format, route contract, redaction, and stale DE fallback config guards.
Steps.step-3: Add an operator-live smoke that checks one active assignment end-to-end: DB assignment, policy health/count, RU HAProxy route, DE fake-TLS accept, and Telegram downstream reachability.
Steps.step-4: Fix DE runtime stale fallback handling so DE_MTPROTO_DOMAIN_FRONTING converges to 127.0.0.1:9443 during deploy without redesigning deploy/install scripts.
Steps.step-5: Improve user cabinet MTProto connection UX: primary Telegram open action, full-link copy, compact secondary manual fields, and clearer degraded/pending states.
Steps.step-6: Add redacted admin/operator diagnostics for masked SNI, policy apply state, fake-TLS path status, downstream status, and safe failure codes.
Steps.step-7: Run local verification: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, backend MTProto tests, frontend build, and Phase-39 static smoke.
Steps.step-8: Apply the narrow fix to RU/DE, run Phase-39 live smoke, then have the operator retest Telegram Desktop using the refreshed primary Telegram action.
Steps.step-9: Record Telegram Desktop available proof and update Phase-38/Phase-39 live status only after the client-level proof passes.

Verification.command-4: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_de_edge_contract.py tests/test_deploy_phase38_static.py tests/test_mtproto_availability_diagnostics.py -v
Verification.command-6: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-051.xml docs/plans/Phase-39.xml docs/verification/V-M-051.xml && mygrace lint

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Policy health alone is no longer treated as MTProto availability proof.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: One active assignment can be proven with redacted live smoke from DB row to fake-TLS accept through the public route.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: DE runtime stale fallback port 8443 is removed or corrected to 9443 by deploy guard.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: User dashboard connection action reduces manual secret-copy errors and remains compact on mobile.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-5: Telegram Desktop marks the issued proxy available before MTProto edge is promoted to stable-live.

Risks.risk-1: Telegram Desktop may cache an old or manually entered bad secret; UX must guide the operator/user to use the freshly generated primary link.
Risks.risk-2: Live smoke can leak secrets if implemented with shell tracing or raw .env output; redaction is a hard gate.
Risks.risk-3: Changing edge fallback ports incorrectly can break HTTPS fallback or admin 8443; Phase-39 guards must distinguish private 9443 fallback from public admin 8443.
Risks.risk-4: Downstream Telegram reachability can vary by region/provider; diagnostics must separate route/secret failures from Telegram DC failures.

AutonomousExecutionSafety.safe-local: Docs, static smoke, backend fake/private-adapter tests, frontend build, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.requires-operator: Live RU/DE smoke, server-side deploy application, and Telegram Desktop availability proof.
